@startuml
skinparam classAttributeIconSize 0

package "javafx" {
    [Stage] as Stage <<C>> 
}

[Scene] as Scene <<C>> 

class BorderPane <<C>> {
    
}

MenuBar --|> MenuBar : north
TabPane -d- TabPane : center


TreeFileManagerView --> TreeFileManagerView : fileManager



IDiagramMenu <|-- IDiagramEditor 


MenuItem .. MenuItem : items




' Relationships between classes in javafx package
BorderPane *-- "1..n" MenuItem : contains >
BorderPane o--> "*" ILayoutManager : manages >
BorderPanel ->* LayoutStrategy : uses strategy pattern for layout management logic delegation purposes here too! This allows flexibility by changing strategies dynamically if needed later on without altering core functionality much at all since they're decoupled via interfaces rather than concrete implementations directly tied together tightly coupled otherwise which would make future changes harder/more error prone overall design wise anyway so best practice really dictates doing things this way whenever possible especially given how often requirements change over time during development cycles etc... Anyway moving forward now let's talk about what happens next after setting up these basic structures first before diving deeper into more complex interactions among them soon enough hopefully once everything else falls neatly place accordingly alrighty then shall we proceed further ahead?
